entries.title: Understanding Neural Networks
entries.content:
Neural networks are computational models inspired by biological neurons. They consist of interconnected nodes that process information through weighted connections.

Key Components:

  • Input layer - receives raw data
  • Hidden layers - process information
  • Output layer - produces results
  • Activation functions - introduce non-linearity

The training process uses backpropagation to adjust weights based on error signals, enabling the network to learn complex patterns from data.

entries.title: The Psychology of Money
entries.content:
Money is more about psychology than mathematics. Our relationship with wealth is shaped by our past experiences and emotional patterns.

Key Insights:

  • Wealth is invisible - true net worth is rarely on display
  • The key factor is how much you save, not how much you earn
  • Financial success requires a long time horizon
  • Luck and risk play larger roles than we admit

Understanding our behavioral biases around money is crucial for building long-term financial security.

entries.title: Building Scalable Systems
entries.content:
Scalability is about designing systems that can handle growth without fundamental redesign. There are two primary approaches to scaling:

Vertical Scaling: Adding more resources to a single server - limited by hardware constraints.

Horizontal Scaling: Adding more servers to distribute load - more complex but virtually unlimited.

Effective strategies include load balancing, database sharding, caching layers, and asynchronous processing. The goal is to decouple components and reduce dependencies.

entries.title: Principles of Effective Leadership
entries.content:
Leadership is the ability to influence others toward a common goal. The most effective leaders combine clarity, empathy, and accountability.

Core Principles:

  • Lead by example - your actions speak louder than words
  • Communicate clearly - ambiguity breeds confusion
  • Develop your team - invest in people's growth
  • Make decisive decisions - indecision creates chaos
  • Take ownership of failures - create psychological safety

Great leaders understand that their primary job is to enable their teams to do their best work.

entries.title: The Art of Compound Growth
entries.content:
Compound growth is one of the most powerful forces in nature and finance. Small, consistent improvements compound into extraordinary results over time.

A 1% improvement each day results in a 37x improvement in a year. This principle applies to:

  • Learning and skill development
  • Financial wealth through investing
  • Business growth through customer satisfaction
  • Personal health through consistent habits

The key is patience and consistency. Most people overestimate what they can achieve in a year but underestimate what they can achieve in a decade.

entries.title: Small Ideas, Big Momentum
entries.content:
Sometimes the best ideas do not arrive as grand revelations. They arrive as small sparks that deserve to be captured and explored.

Why this matters:

  • Momentum often starts with one tiny experiment
  • Capturing ideas early prevents them from disappearing
  • Shared ideas can become collaborative projects

Keep a habit of writing down the fragments that feel interesting, even if they are not fully formed yet.
